title: Interactive Voice Story | Enter a Plot and Choose a Role
metaDescription: See how an interactive voice story can begin with an existing plot, an available character, and a clear first scene to perform.
heroMarkdown: An interactive voice story gives a performer a clear way into a scene: begin with a plot already in motion, choose an available character, and respond from inside that role. It is not a request to invent a world before the first line. The story supplies a situation, while the role gives you a point of view for entering it.

sections.heading: Start with the moment, not a blank prompt
sections.bodyMarkdown: The useful first question is not how much background you can write. It is what has already happened in the opening scene. An existing plot gives an interactive voice story a setting, an immediate situation, and a reason for a character to speak. The product is an AI voice cosplay and roleplay experience built around acting inside a story. That story-led format lets you arrive at a scene with something to notice or answer, rather than facing an empty box and trying to build every piece of context alone.

sections.heading: Choose a role to find your point of view
sections.bodyMarkdown: Once you understand the premise, character choice makes the scene personal. Each playable experience starts from an existing story plot and offers the characters available in that plot. Choose the role you want to perform, then consider what that person knows, wants, or needs in the current moment. The same scene can invite different responses depending on the role. This is a practical distinction from generic character chat: the choice belongs to a specific story situation, not to a detached profile with no opening context.

sections.heading: Use the opening scene as a performance cue
sections.bodyMarkdown: A prepared plot does not need to dictate every line. It gives your first response an anchor. Read the immediate situation, imagine what the selected character would notice first, and answer from that perspective. In a voice roleplay format, the scene becomes a cue for acting inside the fiction. You can focus on the tension that is already present instead of treating the first turn as a writing assignment. The goal is a clear entry into an original story, not an invented technical promise about how voice works.

sections.heading: Why plot, role, and participation belong together
sections.bodyMarkdown: The team has previously built AI interactive-fiction and simulator experiences, which informs a format where story context and character participation meet. A plot provides the opening conditions. A role provides a perspective. Your performance provides the response inside that shared scene. Keeping these pieces together makes the experience easier to understand: you are entering a story as an available character, not choosing an unrelated persona and then supplying the entire premise yourself. The description stays with original story material and does not depend on borrowed characters, worlds, or trademarks.

sections.heading: A simple three-step way to begin
sections.bodyMarkdown: First, open the story and take in its opening situation. Second, review the available characters and choose the role you want to perform. Third, enter the scene with that character's point of view in mind. That sequence is the original contribution of an interactive voice story: premise, role, then performance. For the broader format, see the AI voice roleplay story overview. If you want a closer look at why a prepared premise helps, read the story based AI roleplay guide before returning to choose a scene.

faqs.question: What is an interactive voice story?
faqs.answerMarkdown: It is a story-led way to enter an AI voice cosplay and roleplay experience. You begin with an existing plot, select an available character, and perform from inside the scene.

faqs.question: Do I need to write the premise before I begin?
faqs.answerMarkdown: No. Each playable experience starts from an existing story plot rather than an empty chat box. The opening situation provides context before you choose a role.

faqs.question: How do I choose a role for the scene?
faqs.answerMarkdown: Review the characters available in the featured story and choose the one you want to perform. The choice gives you a point of view for the opening situation.

faqs.question: Does this use familiar fictional franchises?
faqs.answerMarkdown: No. The page describes original story material and avoids unlicensed entertainment characters, worlds, and trademarks.
